goal: Parallel run: DB schema + Go models for dual-version execution tracking

acceptance_criteria.description: Migration 045 creates migration.parallel_run table with columns: run_id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), engagement_id UUID NOT NULL FK to migration.engagement, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING' CHECK (PENDING|RUNNING|PAUSED|COMPLETED|FAILED|CANCELLED), legacy_source TEXT NOT NULL (identifies the legacy system endpoint), canonical_source TEXT NOT NULL (identifies the new system endpoint), comparison_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SAMPLE' CHECK (SAMPLE|FULL|CONTINUOUS) — note: CONTINUOUS requires CDC integration not yet built, add SQL comment documenting this, sample_rate NUMERIC(5,4) DEFAULT 0.1000, started_by TEXT NOT NULL, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). Index on engagement_id for RLS subquery performance and listing. RLS policy uses the established Tier B pattern: USING (engagement_id IN (SELECT engagement_id FROM migration.engagement WHERE tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id', true)::UUID)).

Migration 045 also creates migration.parallel_run_result table with columns: result_id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), run_id UUID NOT NULL FK to migration.parallel_run, member_id VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL (matches reconciliation table pattern), canonical_entity TEXT NOT NULL, field_name TEXT NOT NULL, legacy_value TEXT, new_value TEXT, match BOOLEAN NOT NULL, variance_amount NUMERIC(15,2), variance_pct NUMERIC(8,4), checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). UNIQUE constraint on (run_id, member_id, canonical_entity, field_name) for idempotent inserts. Indexes on (run_id, match) for mismatch filtering and (run_id, canonical_entity) for entity-level summaries. RLS policy joins through parallel_run to engagement to tenant: USING (run_id IN (SELECT run_id FROM migration.parallel_run WHERE engagement_id IN (SELECT engagement_id FROM migration.engagement WHERE tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id', true)::UUID))).

Go model types defined in models/parallel_run.go: ParallelRun struct, ParallelRunResult struct, ParallelRunStatus enum (PENDING|RUNNING|PAUSED|COMPLETED|FAILED|CANCELLED), ComparisonMode enum (SAMPLE|FULL|CONTINUOUS), ParallelRunSummary struct with fields: TotalCompared int, MatchCount int, MismatchCount int, MatchRatePct float64, ByEntity map[string]EntityMatchSummary. EntityMatchSummary struct has MatchCount and MismatchCount fields. All build cleanly.

DB query functions in db/parallel_run.go: CreateParallelRun, GetParallelRun, ListParallelRuns (by engagement_id, with optional status filter), UpdateParallelRunStatus, InsertParallelRunResults (batch insert using ON CONFLICT (run_id, member_id, canonical_entity, field_name) DO NOTHING for idempotent retries), GetParallelRunResults (with pagination + match filter, per_page max 200), GetParallelRunSummary (aggregate COUNT/SUM by entity via SQL, not client-side). All use scanParallelRun/scanParallelRunResult DRY helpers following the engagement.go scanner pattern.

ParallelRunStatus has valid transition enforcement: PENDING→RUNNING, RUNNING→PAUSED|COMPLETED|FAILED|CANCELLED, PAUSED→RUNNING|CANCELLED. Invalid transitions return error. Tested with at least 3 valid and 3 invalid transitions.

Untyped string constant added to jobqueue/queue.go: const JobTypeParallelRun = "parallel_run". This follows the existing pattern where job types are free-form strings — the constant provides a canonical reference without introducing a typed enum that would break existing callers using raw string literals.

critical: RLS uses Tier B pattern via current_setting('app.tenant_id') — NOT engagement_members (which does not exist in this codebase)
engagement_id NOT NULL and FK to migration.engagement on parallel_run table
parallel_run_result joins through parallel_run to get tenant scoping — two-hop RLS subquery
variance_amount uses NUMERIC(15,2), not float — monetary precision required for benefit comparisons
Status transitions enforced in model layer — no direct string assignment
UNIQUE constraint on (run_id, member_id, canonical_entity, field_name) for idempotent batch inserts
high: Index on engagement_id for parallel_run table (consistent with all engagement-scoped tables, required for RLS subquery)
Index on (run_id, match) for efficient mismatch filtering
Index on (run_id, canonical_entity) for entity-level grouping
Batch insert for results (not row-by-row) with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
Summary query uses COUNT/SUM aggregation, not client-side counting
Tests use sqlmock-compatible patterns (scanner interface, explicit column lists)
rls_test.go updated: parallel_run and parallel_run_result added to allMigrationTables
medium: sample_rate default 0.1 (10%) is sensible for initial parallel runs
comparison_mode CONTINUOUS documented as future-only in SQL comment and Go model comment
